id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0002
text: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). ![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/stressed-man-sitting-at-workdesk-at-office-2075654179-landscape-16x9.jpg?t=h320). 21/05/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). An overwhelming majority of companies have already faced a cyberattack. Yet entrepreneurs tend to underestimate the associated risks. Often only after an organisation has suffered damage, for example lost revenue or financial costs for system recovery, do they recognise the risks. Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0002

metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0003
text: An overwhelming majority of companies have already faced a cyberattack. Yet entrepreneurs tend to underestimate the associated risks. Often only after an organisation has suffered damage, for example lost revenue or financial costs for system recovery, do they recognise the risks. Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0003

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0004
text: Yet entrepreneurs tend to underestimate the associated risks. Often only after an organisation has suffered damage, for example lost revenue or financial costs for system recovery, do they recognise the risks. Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0004

metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0005
text: Often only after an organisation has suffered damage, for example lost revenue or financial costs for system recovery, do they recognise the risks. Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year. More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0005
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0006
text: Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year. More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted. The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0006
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0007
text: Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year. More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted. The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals. The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0007
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0008
text: In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year. More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted. The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals. The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0008
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0009
text: More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted. The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals. The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0009
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0010
text: The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals. The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly. Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0010
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0011
text: The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly. Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year. This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0011

text: Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly. Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year. This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year. Higher risk assessment after damage.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0012
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0013
text: In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly. Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year. This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year. Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0013
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0014
text: Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year. This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year. Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0014

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0015
text: This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year. Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack. Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0015
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0016
text: Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack. Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher. Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0016
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0017
text: Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack. Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher. Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation. Of the companies that have not yet suffered damage from an attack, only 26 per cent share this risk assessment.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0017

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0018
text: This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack. Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher. Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation. Of the companies that have not yet suffered damage from an attack, only 26 per cent share this risk assessment. This is comparable to the risk assessment of companies that have never been attacked at all.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0018

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0019
text: Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher. Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation. Of the companies that have not yet suffered damage from an attack, only 26 per cent share this risk assessment. This is comparable to the risk assessment of companies that have never been attacked at all. Even so, CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX also sees that experiences of similar companies can serve as a wake-up call. “Entrepreneurs who hear from their neighbour on the business park about an unpleasant cyberattack are more inclined to start looking at solutions.

text:
Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation. Of the companies that have not yet suffered damage from an attack, only 26 per cent share this risk assessment. This is comparable to the risk assessment of companies that have never been attacked at all. Even so, CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX also sees that experiences of similar companies can serve as a wake-up call. “Entrepreneurs who hear from their neighbour on the business park about an unpleasant cyberattack are more inclined to start looking at solutions. They do not walk away with a contract straight away, but they are at least more aware of the risks.”
Measures to reduce risks.

They do not walk away with a contract straight away, but they are at least more aware of the risks.”
Measures to reduce risks. Entrepreneurs would do well to protect themselves against cyberattacks. You can do this in three relatively simple steps. The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0023

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0024
text:
They do not walk away with a contract straight away, but they are at least more aware of the risks.”
Measures to reduce risks. Entrepreneurs would do well to protect themselves against cyberattacks. You can do this in three relatively simple steps. The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order. The second step is to take measures, such as promoting safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns certain data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0024

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__c5-sentence-window__0025
text: Entrepreneurs would do well to protect themselves against cyberattacks. You can do this in three relatively simple steps. The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order. The second step is to take measures, such as promoting safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns certain data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers. Finally, it is essential to develop procedures to detect and handle cyber incidents.
